spec_natural_language: function initialize MUST NOT succeed when called directly on the implementation contract (i.e., when implementation contract is already deployed but not accessed through proxy)

violated_invariant.precondition: The implementation contract constructor has been executed (contract is deployed)
violated_invariant.expected_behavior: Calling initialize on the implementation contract directly should revert because _disableInitializers() should have been called in the constructor

extraction_reasoning: The finding clearly describes that the initialize() function on the implementation contract can be called by anyone because there is no constructor calling _disableInitializers(). The invariant is straightforward: implementation contracts using UUPS pattern must disable initialization in constructor to prevent direct initialization attacks.
